pytorch.reason: All density matrix states, quantum channels, entropy computations (von Neumann, fiber phase, chirality, off-diagonal norm, I_c, quantum discord) run as torch.complex128 tensors; all 7 coupling tests (L1_on_L1 through L3_on_L5) are pytorch-native

pyg.reason: not needed -- no message passing in this sim

z3.reason: Encodes formal incompatibility: fiber entropy requires coherence; z-dephasing destroys coherence; UNSAT proves L3 operator is formally incompatible with L1 entropy measurement

cvc5.reason: not needed -- z3 covers incompatibility proof

sympy.reason: Symbolic derivation showing rho_01 after Z-dephasing = (1-2p)*c*s*e^{-i*phi}; at p=0.5 this vanishes identically, removing all phi (fiber phase) information; proves fiber entropy collapses to zero after full Z-dephasing

clifford.reason: Verifies L1 SU(2) rotor is correctly constructed as a Cl(3) bivector exponential; confirms R*R.reverse()=1 (unit rotor) and rotation formula R*v*R.rev gives correct rotation of basis vectors; grounding the SU(2) fiber automorphism in geometric algebra

geomstats.reason: not needed -- Hopf geometry done via Cl(3) rotors

e3nn.reason: not needed -- equivariance handled via Clifford SU(2)

rustworkx.reason: Directed graph where edge L_i->L_j means PRESERVE or ENHANCE coupling; SCCs identify mutually compatible layer pairs; determines which layers can coexist without degrading each other's entropy

xgi.reason: not needed -- coupling structure is a directed graph, not hypergraph

toponetx.reason: not needed -- layer topology captured by rustworkx DAG

gudhi.reason: not needed -- no persistence required for 5x5 coupling matrix

L1_on_L1.note: Z-rotation shifts phi uniformly; equatorial ensemble stays uniform; fiber entropy preserved

L3_on_L1.note: Complete dephasing (p=0.5) collapses off-diagonal -> phi unreadable -> fiber entropy lost

L1_on_L3.expected: ENHANCE (or DEGRADE depending on initial state interpretation)
L1_on_L3.note: X-rotation on Z-diagonal state creates off-diagonal coherences; dephasing metric changes

L2_on_L3.expected: PRESERVE (Z-axis maps to itself under X⊗X: |00><->|11> is still diagonal)
L2_on_L3.note: CPT as X⊗X permutes diagonal entries; keeps state diagonal; phase-damping entropy preserved

L5_on_L4.note: Depolarizing output C destroys A-C entanglement directly; I(A;C) decreases. Note: depolarizing B alone is invariant to I(A;C) by quantum data processing (tracing out B leaves rho_AC unchanged). Must depolarize A or C to affect I(A;C).

L4_on_L2.expected: DEGRADE (relay mixes L/R labels when SWAP is partial)
L4_on_L2.note: Partial SWAP mixes |LL> and |RR> components; chirality entropy should degrade

L3_on_L5.expected: DEGRADE (dephasing destroys quantum correlations; discord reduces)
L3_on_L5.note: Z-dephasing eliminates off-diagonal coherences; quantum discord should decrease

neg_L3_on_L1_identity.note: Dephasing at p=0 is identity; fiber entropy must not change

neg_L5_full_depolarize_kills_discord.expected: DESTROY (maximally mixed state has zero discord)
neg_L5_full_depolarize_kills_discord.note: I/4 is a product state; all quantum correlations vanish

boundary_werner_threshold.note: p=1/3 is separability boundary; discord should be near-zero but not exactly 0

boundary_hopf_polar.note: Near-polar Hopf states have degenerate fiber; lower entropy expected

boundary_bridge_relay_extremes.note: I_c should be monotonic in relay parameter (higher relay = stronger A-C coupling)

L2_on_L2.note: not directly tested; native ops preserve native entropy by definition

L3_on_L3.note: not directly tested; native ops preserve native entropy by definition

L4_on_L4.note: not directly tested; native ops preserve native entropy by definition

L5_on_L5.note: not directly tested; native ops preserve native entropy by definition

L3_operator_incompatible_with_L1_entropy.note: Formally: dephasing destroys coherence, coherence required for fiber entropy -> UNSAT

L5_full_depolarizing_incompatible_with_d.note: Formally: full depolarizing kills quantum correlations; discord cannot survive

test_10_sympy_dephasing_formula.conclusion: At p=0.5: rho_01 = (1-2*0.5)*cos(t/2)*sin(t/2)*e^{-i*phi} = 0. Off-diagonal element vanishes entirely, removing all phi information. The fiber phase phi is completely inaccessible from the dephased state. Fiber entropy collapses: from H(phi)=log(N) to H(phi)=0 (constant, no phi info). This CONFIRMS L3-on-L1 = DESTROY.

clifford_rotor_verification.note: Cl(3) rotor R=cos(a/2)-sin(a/2)*e12 correctly rotates e1 in the e12-plane
